rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HERMAN F. JOHNSON, SR. REVOCABLE 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion; Mmorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Herman F. Johnson, Jr. In Support Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three, And Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Objector Herman F. Johnson, Jr.'s Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set Three, and for Monetary Sanctions. The Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set Three, and Monetary Sanctions is DENIED as moot. Petitioner/Respondent Belinda Robinson timely served responses to the interrogatories before Objector Johnson filed his motion to compel. Johnson's request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Robinson shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HERMAN F. JOHNSON, SR. REVOCABLE 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer To Petitioner Herman F. Johnsons Petition To Invalidate The Herman F. Johnson Sr. Revocable 2010 Trust, Dated July 30, 2010 For Undue Influence, Lack Of Capacity And Constructive Trust (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Discussion: The issue on a demurrer is whether, assuming as true the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed, do the challenged claims necessarily fail. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311,318.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.) A demurrer must demonstrate no right to any relief under the cause of action. (See Augustine, supra, 124 Cal.App.2d at p. 236.) The running of the statutory time limit must appear "clearly and affirmatively" from the face of the complaint. (Committee for Green Foothills v. Santa Clara County Bd. Of Supervisors (2010) 48 Cal.4th 32, 42; Stueve Bros. Farms, LLC v. Berger Kahn (2013) 222 Cal. App.4th 303, 321.) Per the Notice of Hearing, the Demurrer is brought pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10 ("pleading does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action"), based on the Petition being an untimely challenge to the Trust under Probate Code section 16061.8. Under Probate Code section 16061.8, any action to contest the validity of a trust must be filed no more than 120 days after service of the statutory trustee's notice. The declaration of Brian Trowbridge, Robinson's former attorney, filed in support of the Demurrer states that Trowbridge served Johnson the Trustee's Notice under Probate Code section 16061.8 on January 27, 2022. The notice letter attached as Exhibit A to the declaration includes the required advisement language of the statutory time limit in which to bring an action to challenge the trust. 120 days from January 27, 2022, was May 27, 2022. However, facts about this notice are not plead on the face of the Petition and thus the Court cannot consider this evidence in ruling on a demurrer. Johnson shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HERMAN F. JOHNSON, SR. REVOCABLE 2010 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Demurrer To Petitioner Herman F. Johnsons? Petition To Invalidate The Herman F. Johnson Sr. Revocable 2010 Trust, Dated July 30, 2010 For Undue Influence, Lack Of Capacity And Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondent Belinda Robinson's Demurrer to Petitioner Herman F. Johnson's Petition to Invalidate Revocable 2010 Trust, Dated July 30, 2010 for Undue Influence, Lack of Capacity and Constructive Trust. Petitioning party Herman F. Johnson, Jr. filed a late Opposition to this Demurrer on May 11, 2023. Demurring party Belinda F. Robinson filed Notice of Non-Opposition the same day. The Joint Case Management Statement filed May 12, 2023, indicates Robinson expected Johnson to file an Opposition to the Demurrer (at 6:18). Both parties are cautioned, procedural defects in filings may properly be the basis to deny a motion. Statutory procedural requirements must be followed by all parties to an action, as a function of due process and the efficient function of the court. The Court exercises its discretion to consider all the papers filed in this demurrer proceeding and enter a substantive ruling. Johnson's objection to the Demurrer as untimely pursuant to California Rules of Court, Rule 3.2224 is OVERRULED. Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 3.2224 applies to responses to petitions made under the California Environmental Quality Act. Based on the Proof of Service of the Petition to Invalidate the Herman Johnson, Sr. Revocable 2010 Trust for Undue Influence and Lack of Capacity; Constructive Trust by certified mail, and calculation under Code of Civil Procedure section 1005, the Demurrer is timely. Robinson, as the demurring party, failed to comply with the meet and confer requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41. Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a) requires meet and confer conferences prior to filing a demurrer to be either in-person or telephonic: "Before filing a demurrer pursuant to this chapter, the demurring party shall meet and confer in person or by telephone with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to demurrer for the purpose of determining whether an agreement can be reached that would resolve the objections to be raised in the demurrer." Per Houman Chitsaz's sworn declaration in support of the demurrer, he attempted meet and confer efforts with the opposing party only over email, and "did not do so over the phone as that in my opinion was not reasonably practical nor would it be productive." (Chitsaz Decl. par. 2.) The Code requires parties to meet and confer in person or telephonically, not through e-mail. While "A determination by the court that the meet and confer process was insufficient shall not be grounds to overrule or sustain a demurrer" (Code of Civil Procedure section 430.41(a)(4)), the parties and their counsel are instructed to be apprised of the applicable law and to follow statutory procedures. For the reasons discussed below, Belinda F. Robinson's Demurrer to Petitioner Herman F. Johnson's Petition to Invalidate Revocable 2010 Trust, Dated July 30, 2010 for Undue Influence, Lack of Capacity and Constructive Trust is OVERRULED. [Part 1 of 2]
